q:
have
 She ___ a big breakfast yesterday.
a: She had a big breakfast yesterday.

q:
walk
 She ___ to work yesterday.
a: She walked to work yesterday.

q:
take
 It ___ her about half an hour to get to work.
a: It took her about half an hour to get to work.

q:
start
 She ___ work at 8.45 yesterday.
a: She started work at 8.45 yesterday.

q:
have
 She ___ (negative) lunch yesterday.
a: She didn't have lunch yesterday.

q:
finish
 She ___ work at 5 o'clock yesterday.
a: She finished work at 5 o'clock yesterday.

q:
be
 She ___ tired when she got home.
a: She was tired when she got home.

q:
cook
 She ___ a meal yesterday evening.
a: She cooked a meal yesterday evening.

q:
go
 She ___ (negative) out yesterday evening.
a: She didn't go out yesterday evening.

q:
go
 She ___ to bed at 11 o'clock.
a: She went to bed at 11 o'clock.

q:
sleep
 She ___ well last night.
a: She slept well last night.

q:
teach
 'How did you learn to drive?' 'My father ___ me.'
a: My father taught me.

q:
sell
 We couldn't afford to keep our car, so we ___ it.
a: We couldn't afford to keep our car, so we sold it.

q:
fall / hurt
 Dave ___ down the stairs this morning and ___ his leg.
a: Dave fell down the stairs this morning and hurt his leg.

q:
throw / catch
 Joe ___ the ball to Sue, who ___ it.
a: Joe threw the ball to Sue, who caught it.

q:
spend / buy / cost
 Kate ___ a lot of money yesterday. She ___ a dress which ___ £100.
a: Kate spent a lot of money yesterday. She bought a dress which cost £100.

q:
question transformation
 (you / go / by car?)
 'How ___?' 'Yes, we hired a car in San Francisco.'
a: How did you go? By car?

q:
question transformation
 (it / take / how long?)
 'How long ___?' 'Two weeks.'
a: How long did it take?

q:
question transformation
 (you / stay / in hotels?)
 'Where ___? In hotels?' 'Yes, small hotels or motels.'
a: Where did you stay? In hotels?

q:
question transformation
 (the weather / be / good?)
 '___ the weather ___?' 'Yes, but it was very hot.'
a: Was the weather good?

q:
question transformation
 (you / see / the Grand Canyon?)
 '___ you ___ the Grand Canyon?' 'Of course. It was wonderful.'
a: Did you see the Grand Canyon?

q:
disturb
 I knew Sarah was busy, so I ___ (negative) her.
a: I knew Sarah was busy, so I didn't disturb her.

q:
leave
 We were very tired, so we ___ the party early.
a: We were very tired, so we left the party early.

q:
be
 It was hard carrying the bags. They ___ really heavy.
a: It was hard carrying the bags. They were really heavy.

q:
sleep
 The bed was very uncomfortable. I ___ (negative) well.
a: The bed was very uncomfortable. I didn't sleep well.

q:
cost
 This watch wasn't expensive. It ___ (negative) much.
a: This watch wasn't expensive. It didn't cost much.

q:
fly
 The window was open and a bird ___ into the room.
a: The window was open and a bird flew into the room.

q:
have
 I was in a hurry, so I ___ (negative) time to call you.
a: I was in a hurry, so I didn't have time to call you.

q:
be
 I didn't like the hotel. The room ___ (negative) very clean.
a: I didn't like the hotel. The room wasn't very clean.
